text.fullText: From: Charlie.solberg@everyactioncustom.com To: OW-Docket <OW-Docket@epa.gov> Date: 2/17/2026 10:56:29 AM Subject: Docket ID# EPA–HQ–OW–2025–2929 — Opposition to Proposed 2026 Changes to Circumscribe Certification Authority of States and Tribes -------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------- [You don't often get email from charlie.solberg@everyactioncustom.com. Learn why this is important at https://aka.ms/LearnAboutSenderIdentification ] Caution: This email originated from outside EPA, please exercise additional caution when deciding whether to open attachments or click on provided links. Dear Administrator Lee Zeldin, I am a republican who is on the fence regarding allowing easier access to mining in Northern Minnesota. On one side, I have gone canoeing 60 years in Boundary Waters and look forward to camping the same way I did the first year - able to drink and swim in the same water. You ought to try it. If you are one who likes nature and can "rough it", you will really appreciate the experience and will look forward to your total family enjoying things that need to be saved for future generations. On the other side, I talk with my outfitter in Ely, MN about where his priority is today. Even though the outfitter earns money from his work, he was on the side of the miners. I was totally surprised, but he said that his high school graduating class was around 48 students compared to less than 20 students today - "we are dying up here today" he said. It is very hard to go against the indigenous people that live, built and made America great, but as Trump says there should be a deal. Don't change the existing laws with the States and Native Tribes, strengthen those laws that pertain to lowering if not outright banning the use of certain chemicals that endanger the environment - both sides can win. I know that every time my business had to reduce or omit a certain chemical from a process or component, we always found a way, one that was already available, those products just cost more money. Keep up the tariffs, the US cannot compete with the rest of the emerging world when they pollute everything. This a partisan issue, one that will continue to divide the US. Please help change our course. My contact information is included. Thanks for reading, Charlie Solberg Sincerely, Charlie Solberg 204 Warwick Rd Tower Lakes, IL 60010-1275 Charlie.solberg@solbergmfg.com
